text: You know that afternoon slump, where you suddenly can't care about anything? It has a name, 1,600 years ago, Desert monks called it acedia, the noonday Demon, a literal demon they believed attacked them at lunchtime. And what happened to that demon next is why you feel guilty for resting today.
